points.text: COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected people.
Transmission occurs when infectious particles are breathed in or contact the eyes, nose, or mouth.
The risk of transmission is highest at close range but can occur over longer distances indoors.
COVID-19 can spread from touching contaminated surfaces and then touching the face.
People can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms and remain contagious for up to 20 days.
COVID-19 spreads mainly through the air in tiny droplets when people cough, sneeze, breathe, sing, or talk.
The virus can land on the face of a nearby person after a sneeze or cough, or be inhaled in areas with low airflow.
COVID-19 can spread from someone who is infected but has no symptoms.
Risk of catching COVID-19 increases with close contact, poor airflow, and activities like talking or sharing a car.
Living with someone who has COVID-19 or spending time in crowded spaces increases the risk of infection.
COVID-19 spreads through droplets from an infected person's mouth or nose when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing, or breathe.
The virus can be transmitted via larger respiratory droplets and smaller aerosols.
People can spread COVID-19 even if they are not showing symptoms.
COVID-19 can spread by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after contacting contaminated surfaces.
Research is ongoing to understand the most risky situations for COVID-19 spread.
